mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak consumer spending expectations trigger equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in over 50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening factory output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 65% of months where INDPRO increases by more than 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 24-month rolling window or inflation spikes coincide with positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in 55% of months where payroll growth surpasses 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weak final demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence yields positive 20-day returns in over 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, forward guidance and quantitative easing announcements override traditional rate signals, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week when policy rates remain pinned near zero.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 70% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent.
